items.title: The pain Keldon Johnson experienced from losing in the NBA Finals and from struggling throughout most of the postseason didn't stop him from exuding his trademark positive vibes.
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
The pain Keldon Johnson experienced from losing in the NBA Finals and from struggling throughout most of the postseason didn't stop him from exuding his trademark positive vibes.

An emotional Johnson on what he learned from his first postseason:

media.title: What the Spurs' Keldon Johnson had to say about his playoff struggles
media.description: The Sixth Man of the Year averaged just 4.4 points per game in the five games against the Knicks after averaging 13.2 in the regular season.

items.title: Late Wednesday night, after the Spurs had suffered unmitigated heartbreak at the Garden, Keldon Johnson approached each player remaining in the locker room and whispered a single word:
items.author: Jeff McDonald
items.body_excerpt:
Late Wednesday night, after the Spurs had suffered unmitigated heartbreak at the Garden, Keldon Johnson approached each player remaining in the locker room and whispered a single word:

"Believe."

Belief is all the Spurs have left. It still might not be enough.

www.expressnews.com/sports/spurs...

media.title: One word sums up the Spurs' approach to a do-or-die Game 5: 'Believe.'
media.description: Down 3-1 in the NBA Finals after blowing an historic lead in Game 4, the Spurs' season is on life support as series shifts back to San Antonio.

items.title: The fact that you get to the NBA Finals and genuinely good players like Keldon Johnson become nearly unplayable is just a testament to the level these teams are at.
items.author: Kurt Helin
items.body_excerpt: The fact that you get to the NBA Finals and genuinely good players like Keldon Johnson become nearly unplayable is just a testament to the level these teams are at.

items.title: Spurs notebook includes items on how the Game 3 win coincided with a spike in assists and how Keldon Johnson stepped up in weight class to defend Karl-Anthony Towns.
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
Spurs notebook includes items on how the Game 3 win coincided with a spike in assists and how Keldon Johnson stepped up in weight class to defend Karl-Anthony Towns.

media.title: Spurs ball movement back to normal in NBA Finals Game 3 win over Knicks
media.description: The Spurs were back to normal in Game 3, recording 28 assists in the 115-111 victory over the Knicks on Monday night.

items.title: Keldon Johnson scored just 7 points, but Game 3 was one of his best of the playoffs. In 17 minutes, he was a team-best plus-14 and did a good job muscling up against Towns.
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
Keldon Johnson scored just 7 points, but Game 3 was one of his best of the playoffs. In 17 minutes, he was a team-best plus-14 and did a good job muscling up against Towns.

"I just tried to make it tough on him, make his catches tough, try to make him uncomfortable," he said.

items.title: Big challenge win for the Knicks. Wipes off the Wemby 3 and the possibility of a four-point play, takes a foul off Mitch, puts one on Keldon Johnson, gets possession back.

items.title: I can’t believe I’m saying this but the Spurs really need to get Keldon Johnson back in the game. He’s been their best guy against KAT.
items.author: Erik García Gundersen
items.body_excerpt: I can’t believe I’m saying this but the Spurs really need to get Keldon Johnson back in the game. He’s been their best guy against KAT.

items.title: Two fouls in about 17 seconds for KAT, and he's got four, and is gonna take a seat. Knicks instantly give up an offensive rebound and putback layup to Keldon Johnson, and the Spurs have it down to 8. Big moment here, feels like.
items.author: Dan Devine
items.body_excerpt: Two fouls in about 17 seconds for KAT, and he's got four, and is gonna take a seat. Knicks instantly give up an offensive rebound and putback layup to Keldon Johnson, and the Spurs have it down to 8. Big moment here, feels like.

items.title: Keldon Johnson had plenty of time to digest his latest disappointing playoff outing after Wednesday’s loss.
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
Keldon Johnson had plenty of time to digest his latest disappointing playoff outing after Wednesday’s loss.

"I got a long drive home, so it gives you time to think about some things, clear your mind," he said.

On the Sixth Man of the Year's bid to turn the page:
www.expressnews.com/sports/spurs...

media.title: How Spurs' Keldon Johnson is dealing with poor Game 1 outing
media.description: The NBA's Sixth Man of the Year is eager to turn the page on his three-point performance in a playoff-low eight minutes in Wednesday's 105-95 loss to the Knicks.

items.title: De'Aaron Fox is subbed in for Keldon Johnson, who tries to dap him while looking at the Jumbotron, and instead, he daps Fox's sensitive part.

items.title: Spurs now going with the cross-match — Keldon Johnson on KAT, Wemby on Hart. Has led to multiple Spurs stops already.
